page: Microsoft Azure E48as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory performance. The single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10% via stress-ng, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large-block uncached read and write operations perform at average levels. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, contributing to strong encryption and database operation benchmarks.

While the server demonstrates strong multi-threaded decompression and software compilation capabilities, it exhibits performance limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. LLM inference speed for text generation using a large 70B model is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, though small model inference remains strong. The absence of local storage requires reliance on external network-attached storage. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise applications that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring local disk space or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-10% single-core CPU performance and top-25% multi-core and database execution speeds. However, uncached memory bandwidth is average, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v6 is an Eav6-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v6 is an Eav6-family VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine with forty-eight dedicated vCPUs and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz.
384.0 GB of RAM providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.
Top 10% single-core CPU performance and top 25% database operation benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU, requiring external Premium Storage for data persistence.
Suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, database operations, and multi-threaded processing.
